job_criteria: SENIORITY LEVELMid-Senior levelEMPLOYMENT TYPEFull-timeJOB FUNCTIONEngineering and Information TechnologyINDUSTRIESConstruction, Software Development, and IT Services and IT Consulting
job_description: OUR HIRING PROCESS: * We will review your application against our job requirements. We do not employ machine learning technologies during this phase as we believe every human deserves attention from another human. We do not think machines can evaluate your application quite like our seasoned recruiting professionals—every person is unique. We promise to give your candidacy a fair and detailed assessment. * We may then invite you to submit a video interview for the review of the hiring manager. This video interview is often followed by a test or short project that allows us to determine whether you will be a good fit for the team. * At this point, we will invite you to interview with our hiring manager and/or the interview team. Please note: We do not conduct interviews via text message, Telegram, etc. and we never hire anyone into our organization without having met you face-to-face (or via Zoom). You will be invited to come to a live meeting or Zoom, where you will meet our INFUSE team. * From there on, it’s decision time! If you are still excited to join INFUSE and we like you as much, we will have a conversation about your offer. We do not make offers without giving you the opportunity to speak with us live. INFUSE is committed to complying with applicable data privacy and security lawsand regulations. For more information, please see our Privacy PolicyThis is a “build and lead by doing” role. You will define architecture andstandards, deliver features end to end, and consistently raise the bar forreliability, security, and delivery speed.What You’ll Do: * Harden CI/CD (GitHub Actions or Jenkins); build/test containers and deploy via Helm to AWS (EKS or ECS). * Add lightweight bot protection: rate limits, IP allowlists, and abuse detection hooks. * Observability: monitor latency/error budgets via CloudWatch + Grafana, structured logging, tracing where needed. * Performance tuning: DB indexes, caching strategy, queue throughput, API response times. Responsibilities: * Own architecture decisions and keep it pragmatic: “simple first, scalable where it matters.” * Drive code quality: reviews, standards, branching/release hygiene, definition of done. * Break down work, estimate reliably, unblock the team, and align with PM/BA on scope. * Mentor engineers (frontend + backend) and set a strong engineering culture around clarity and ownership. Requirements: * Experience building backend systems with .NET in production * Hands-on work with PostgreSQL and Redis * Practical experience with React (TypeScript, MUI) * API design and maintenance using OpenAPI 3 * CI/CD experience with GitHub Actions * Experience with queues and schedulers such as SQS, EventBridge with Lambda, or Celery * 3+ years building production systems; 2+ years in a lead/ownership role. * Strong Python backend experience (Flask/FastAPI), including API design, auth, and DB modeling. * Real experience running things in production: CI/CD, Docker, AWS, monitoring, and incidents. * Deep understanding of RBAC, session/auth flows, and security basics (OWASP-minded). * Comfortable with async/event-driven patterns: queues, schedulers, retries, idempotency. * Clear documentation, good tradeoffs, and keeping stakeholders in the loop. Would be a plus: * Grafana + Prometheus + Loki * Good React/TypeScript skills (component architecture, state management, UX for admin tools). * Semantic search/retrieval experience (vector search, RAG patterns, ranking, evaluation). * Experience with OpenSearch/Elasticsearch, pgvector, or similar search stacks. * Familiarity with crawlers/scrapers, content pipelines, and anti-abuse patterns. * Infrastructure as Code (Terraform/CDK), multi-env deployments, feature flags. * Experience designing audit trails, moderation/review workflows, and analytics events. Please feel free to apply and let’s discuss this opportunity.Show more Show less

job_criteria: SENIORITY LEVELMid-Senior levelEMPLOYMENT TYPEFull-timeJOB FUNCTIONEngineeringINDUSTRIESSoftware Development, IT Services and IT Consulting, and PharmaceuticalManufacturing
job_description: Veeva Systems is a mission-driven organization and pioneer in industry cloud,helping life sciences companies bring therapies to patients faster. As one ofthe fastest-growing SaaS companies in history, we surpassed $2B in revenue inour last fiscal year with extensive growth potential ahead.At the heart of Veeva are our values: Do the Right Thing, Customer Success,Employee Success, and Speed. We're not just any public company – we made historyin 2021 by becoming a public benefit corporation (PBC), legally bound tobalancing the interests of customers, employees, society, and investors.As a Work Anywhere company, we support your flexibility to work from home or inthe office, so you can thrive in your ideal environment.Join us in transforming the life sciences industry, committed to making apositive impact on its customers, employees, and communities.The RoleAt Veeva Link, we're building the intelligence layer for life sciences, creatingconnected data applications that accelerate drug development and significantlyimprove patient outcomes. Our core belief: combining the highest quality datawith state-of-the-art software delivers immense value.We believe execution matters most. Progress comes from speed, accuracy, andquality in what we build every day. Our engineering approach emphasizes clearproduct definitions leading to meticulous technical designs. We leverage amodern tech stack to deliver inherently reliable applications and a great userexperience.Our work spans two critical areas:Data-Driven InsightsWe develop sophisticated applications that map and understand global scientificand medical expertise, providing high-impact insights and AI-generatedintelligence. Engineers here tackle complex data modeling, entity resolution,and advanced machine learning.Intelligent Business WorkflowsWe engineer workflow applications powered by our data, using AI to find patternsand route information precisely. This involves designing scalable pipelines andintelligent automation.Why Join Veeva Link?Build products that create significant positive impact in global healthcare.Work in an environment led by a CEO with an engineering background.Work Anywhere means you can work in an office or at home on any given day.Empowered Autonomy: Tech decisions are made within teams, giving engineerssignificant autonomy.Collaborate with highly skilled teammates who take pride in achieving greatoutcomes. Excellence matters.What You'll Do * Build scalable web applications utilizing open-source tools and technologies * Build and use REST and GraphQL APIs * Be able to work independently while communicating progress to other members of the team * Participate in the technical planning, user story/technical acceptance creation, and implementation * Identify and solve complex problems * Plan technical architectures that minimize or eliminate performance problems, complexity, or solution fragility Requirements * 7+ years of professional experience building scalable and reliable web applications is required * 5+ years of experience with Python and Python web frameworks (Django, Flask, FastAPI, etc...) * 5+ years of experience with front-end technologies, such as JavaScript, Typescript, and React * Able to create database schemas that represent and support business processes * Unit test and debugging skills * 3+ years of work experience with RDBMS such as MySQL or PostgreSQL * Independent, self-managed, and motivated * Good command of the English language Nice to Have * Experience with any of the following would be a plus: GraphQL, React, FastAPI, AWS (or any other Cloud provider) * A bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or Information Systems #RemoteSpainVeeva’s headquarters is located in the San Francisco Bay Area with offices inmore than 15 countries around the world.As an equal opportunity employer, Veeva is committed to fostering a culture ofinclusion and growing a diverse workforce. Diversity makes us stronger. It comesin many forms. Gender, race, ethnicity, religion, politics, sexual orientation,age, disability and life experience shape us all into unique individuals. Wevalue people for the individuals they are and the contributions they can bringto our teams.If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability or special need whenapplying for a role or in our recruitment process, please contact us attalent_accommodations@veeva.com.Show more Show less
title: Senior Engineer - Full Stack (Remote)

job_criteria: SENIORITY LEVELDirectorEMPLOYMENT TYPEFull-timeJOB FUNCTIONEngineering, Product Management, and ResearchINDUSTRIESSoftware Development, Financial Services, and IT Services and IT Consulting
job_description: You're a full-stack product engineer seeking ownership instead of taskassignments. This position gives you complete control of an AI product fromstart to finish: weekly customer meetings, product decision-making, andproduction deployments. Your dual mandate: drive Kayako One to resolve supporttickets autonomously and construct an AI sales pipeline that moves prospectsfrom initial contact through deal closure. You own the technology stack, productroadmap, release schedule, and business results.The wider industry isolates engineers from end users behind layers of "process."That's not our approach. No chain of product managers, designers, or customersuccess teams stands between you and ground truth. You will lead weeklydiscovery sessions, convert customer pain into working features, deploy rapidly,and track tangible outcomes. You will also create the AI sales automationframework that may serve as the blueprint for Trilogy's portfolio-widego-to-market motion. Here, your performance is measured by adoption metrics,resolution rates, CSAT scores, and pipeline velocity.This role means full ownership: new AI capabilities in Kayako One that improveticket resolution; platform integrations with CRM and ticketing systems;outreach sequences and targeting rules; pipeline connections; analytics thatvalidate impact; plus deployment, monitoring, and on-call responsibilities tomaintain uptime. This is not a backend-only position executing specs handed downfrom others, nor a comfortable lane with neat handoffs. You'll code as the workdemands, configure AWS infrastructure with confidence, adopt tools likeVoiceflow or Cursor without friction, and make LLMs reliable in production—notjust impressive in demos.You'll operate at the sharp edge of our help desk product and a high-priority AIsales automation initiative, with visibility across the portfolio. Success meanscustomers actively using your releases, shorter resolution times, improvedend-user satisfaction, and a more efficient, faster-moving sales pipeline. Ifyou want to own measurable outcomes, engage customers every week, filter outdistractions, and ship what actually moves metrics, this is your role.What You Will Be Doing * Develop and deploy Kayako One capabilities—AI-driven ticket resolution improvements, CRM and ticketing platform integrations, scalable and dependable infrastructure, plus performance optimizations and defect fixes. * Conduct weekly customer discovery sessions to convert user pain points into feature requirements and confirm product direction with actual users. * Create the AI sales automation infrastructure—outreach sequences, pipeline and tool integrations, automated progression through sales stages, and metrics dashboards. * Manage deployments and operations—end-to-end releases, monitoring/alerting/incident handling, and AWS/database administration. What You Won’t Be Doing * This isn't simply coding against a specification someone else prepared — you define what gets built based on direct customer feedback. * This isn't work that remains in pre-production environments — your code reaches production and serves live customers. * This isn't a narrowly scoped specialty role — you are accountable for the complete stack, underlying infrastructure, and user experience. job_description: About Smart WorkingAt Smart Working, we believe your job should not only look right on paper butalso feel right every day. This isn’t just another remote opportunity - it’sabout finding where you truly belong, no matter where you are. From day one,you’re welcomed into a genuine community that values your growth and well-being.Our mission is simple: to break down geographic barriers and connect skilledprofessionals with outstanding global teams and products for full-time,long-term roles. We help you discover meaningful work with teams that invest inyour success, where you’re empowered to grow personally and professionally.Join one of the highest-rated workplaces on Glassdoor and experience what itmeans to thrive in a truly remote-first world.About the RoleWe are hiring a Senior Full Stack Developer to join a small, collaborativeengineering team working on the evolution of our platform. This is a long-termopportunity with a strong backend focus (70%), supporting key initiativesincluding modernising Python AWS Lambda microservices, improving ingestioncapabilities, and strengthening platform observability. You will work closelywith the Technical Lead, Engineers, Manual Tester, and Technical BA to deliverscalable, well-tested solutions and contribute to future projects, includingrebuilding our phone and web applications to surface more meaningfulconversation insights starting in Q2 2026.Responsibilities * Contribute to the modernisation of Python AWS Lambda microservices by understanding legacy Python code and rebuilding services using the modern SDK * Build and maintain extensive unit test suites to ensure reliability, quality, and long-term maintainability of microservices * Support the rewrite of ingestion capabilities by developing new Python Lambda microservices and extending TypeScript data pipelines * Help rewrite the initial part of the data pipeline to support high-throughput analysis * Improve the observability of conversations flowing through the platform and ensure issues can be identified and diagnosed efficiently * Contribute to the initiative starting Q2 2026 to rebuild both the phone application and web application to surface more meaningful conversation insights * Work effectively within a small team, delivering work both independently and collaboratively * Collaborate with the Technical BA to assist with refining tickets and clarifying requirements before implementation Requirements * 5+ years of professional experience as a Full Stack Developer or similar role * 5+ years of hands-on experience with Python (mandatory) * 4+ years of hands-on experience with TypeScript (core skill; exceptional candidates may be considered without it) * Strong experience working in a full-stack development environment with a backend focus * Strong experience working with serverless and microservice-style architectures * Proven ability to work with legacy codebases and modernise them into scalable, maintainable solutions * Strong unit testing skills and experience building reliable automated test coverage * Strong problem-solving skills with a detail-oriented mindset * Ability to work in a balanced way across collaboration and independent delivery Nice to Have * 2+ years of experience with React (Vue is also acceptable) * 1+ year of experience with AI / LLM-related development or exposure to AI-enabled features * 2+ years of experience with AWS (Azure or GCP also acceptable) * Experience improving observability, monitoring, logging, and troubleshooting in production environments * Experience working on ingestion pipelines, data processing workflows, or high-throughput systems Benefits * Fixed Shifts: 12:00 PM - 9:30 PM IST (Summer) | 1:00 PM - 10:30 PM IST (Winter) * No Weekend Work: Real work-life balance, not just words * Day 1 Benefits: Laptop and full medical insurance provided * Support That Matters: Mentorship, community, and forums where ideas are shared * True Belonging: A long-term career where your contributions are valued At Smart Working, you’ll never be just another remote hire.Be a Smart Worker - valued, empowered, and part of a culture that celebratesintegrity, excellence, and ambition.If that sounds like your kind of place, we’d love to hear your story. job_description: We are seeking a highly technical product leader prepared to drive theAI-powered transformation of education. 2 Hour Learning is building the nextgeneration of learning infrastructure and needs a technical architect to leadthe evolution of our AI-driven ecosystem. This position demands someone with thetechnical depth to function at a CTO level, the strategic vision to catalyzetransformational shifts, and the commitment to deploy those capabilities inservice of unlocking student achievement at massive scale.Where most organizations append AI to conventional educational frameworks as asupplementary layer, 2 Hour Learning has pursued a fundamentally different path:we have reconstructed the school experience from first principles, leveraging AIand learning science to eliminate the limitations inherent in traditionalclassrooms. The results speak for themselves — our students don't merelyprogress, they excel, earning multiple 4s and 5s on AP assessments,demonstrating multi-year growth trajectories within single academic years, andregularly surpassing their own projected outcomes. Our work validates that whenAI serves as the foundational operating system for learning, student performancereaches extraordinary levels.This is not a conventional product management position centered on user feedbackcycles or gradual feature rollouts. This is an executive-level technical productownership role where you will control the architecture, technical roadmap, andAI integration across our complete learning platform. You will make the criticaltechnical trade-offs required to keep our products lean, scalable, andrelentlessly optimized for student outcome improvement. You will operate withthe mindset of a CTO while maintaining the velocity of a product entrepreneur,fusing engineering discipline with learning science principles to consistentlyproduce breakthrough results.If you are prepared to spearhead the technical reinvention of education andpossess the execution discipline to realize that vision, we invite yourapplication. This is your chance to architect the future of learning.What You Will Be Doing * Building and maintaining deep expertise in education product development to inform strategic choices * Creating technical specifications that resolve critical architectural decisions and emphasize simplicity, operational efficiency, and sustainable design * Deploying generative AI capabilities to automate and optimize engineering workflows, supporting the organizational goal of minimizing manual intervention What You Won’t Be Doing * Functioning solely as a people manager; this role prioritizes your business insight and technical expertise * Spending time in endless meetings, committees, or administrative routines; we have eliminated management overhead through automation so you can concentrate on delivering tangible business impact via hands-on contribution * Proposing high-level strategies detached from implementation detail; your guidance must be grounded in product-level understanding Vice President Of Product Engineering Key Responsibilities * Serve as the CTO for one or more education products, holding ownership of foundational competencies and generating business value through flawless execution and strategic leadership Basic Requirements * Significant experience or demonstrated interest in data-driven learning science * 7+ years of experience driving critical architecture and design decisions on software initiatives, including application of architecture design patterns or adoption of major open source technologies * 2+ years of experience making design decisions that involve cloud computing services (primarily AWS) * 4+ years of experience in roles with complete technical and delivery accountability for a revenue-generating product * Proven track record building product features that leverage advanced Generative AI capabilities * Experience applying Generative AI to streamline and simplify operational processes About 2 Hour LearningEducation is broken, but 2 Hour Learning is proving it doesn’t have to be.They’re tearing down the outdated one-size-fits-all model and replacing it withAI-driven personalized learning that helps kids master academics in just twohours a day.With students consistently ranking in the top 1-2% nationally and the top 20%achieving an astonishing 6.5x growth, they’re proving that smarter learning ispossible. At 2 Hour Learning, it’s talent and performance that matter.They offer a dynamic, on-campus and remote-friendly environment whereinnovators, educators, and AI specialists can be a part of fixing a brokenschool system.2 Hour Learning is reprogramming learning for the AI era.Here’s How They’re Fixing It.There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. job_criteria: SENIORITY LEVELExecutiveEMPLOYMENT TYPEFull-timeJOB FUNCTIONEngineering and Information TechnologyINDUSTRIESComputer and Network Security
job_description: DescriptionThe MissionIn cybersecurity, engineering isn’t just building software—it’s building theshield that protects the world’s enterprises from threats they can’t yet see.Exabeam is a six-time Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for SIEM and the largestindependent security operations platform in the market, protecting organizationswith AI-driven behavioral analytics that detect what rules-based systems miss.Our platform processes over 1 million events per second, turns petabytes ofsecurity data into real-time intelligence, and powers the Security OperationsCenters that defend some of the world’s most critical enterprises.We’re looking for a VP of Engineering who leads with trust, builds throughpeople, and creates environments where exceptional engineering happens as anatural consequence of exceptional culture. You’ll lead the organizationresponsible for Threat Center, Automation, Search, Outcomes Navigator, andProduct Quality—translating our technology vision into engineering execution ata pivotal moment in the company’s trajectory.This is not a caretaker role. With agentic AI reshaping security operations andthe SIEM market converging around platform-scale intelligence, the leadershipchallenges ahead are as human as they are technical: building trust withglobally distributed teams, maintaining velocity and morale through rapidgrowth, and developing the next generation of engineering leaders who will carrythis platform forward.About ExabeamExabeam pioneered the application of AI and machine learning to cybersecuritythrough User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA), and today delivers acomprehensive Threat Detection, Investigation, and Response (TDIR) platform thatunifies SIEM, UEBA, and SOAR in a single experience. Built cloud-native onGoogle Cloud Platform, the Exabeam New-Scale platform integrates 795+ behavioralmodels, 1,800+ anomaly rules, and 9,500+ pre-built log parsers to detect insiderthreats, credential abuse, and lateral movement that traditional tools miss.In 2025, we launched Exabeam Nova—an agentic AI system with six specialized AIagents that automates investigation, risk scoring, search, and strategicadvisory across the SOC. We introduced the industry’s first AI Agent BehaviorAnalytics, securing the emerging non-human workforce of enterprise AI agents.And our Outcomes Navigator became the first SIEM capability to offerconfigurable peer benchmarking against the MITRE ATT&CK framework, helping CISOsmeasure and communicate the value of their security programs.We are a PE-backed company (Thoma Bravo) with approximately 850 employees acrosssix continents, operating with the urgency of a growth-stage company and theambition of a market leader. We shipped 400+ product innovations in the pastyear alone.What You’ll OwnPlatform strategy and execution. You will own the engineering roadmap anddelivery for five critical product areas that form the backbone of the Exabeamplatform:Threat CenterOur unified TDIR workbench where customer SOC analysts manage alerts,investigations, and response. You’ll guide the teams building AI-poweredautomation that reconstructs the full story behind every incident throughautomated timelines and behavioral analytics.AutomationOur low-code/no-code SOAR environment integrating with 1,000+ third-party tools.You’ll lead the teams enabling SOC workflow automation, from pre-built playbooksto self-service rule authoring and API-based integrations.SearchNatural language search powered by Google, capable of querying petabytes of datain seconds. You’ll ensure this capability scales while remaining intuitive foranalysts at every skill level.Outcomes NavigatorOur first-of-its-kind capability for measuring security program effectivenessagainst MITRE ATT&CK techniques and compliance frameworks. You’ll advance peerbenchmarking, compliance posture assessment, and executive-ready reporting.Product QualityYou’ll champion engineering excellence across the organization, building theculture, processes, observability practices, and quality frameworks that ensureour platform performs flawlessly when customers need it most.People leadership and organizational health. You’ll lead a globally distributedteam of 50 engineers across the US and India, including partnering withengineering managers, architects, and individual contributors. Your primary jobis to build a leadership bench that multiplies your impact—developingengineering managers into confident, autonomous leaders and creating theorganizational health that allows great work to happen. You’ll design teamstructures that maximize collaboration across time zones while empowering localownership.Customer-obsessed engineering culture. You’ll build direct feedback loopsbetween engineering and the customer experience—connecting production telemetry,support escalations, Customer Success insights, and product analytics to drivecontinuous improvement. Customer SOC analysts work under extraordinary pressure,24/7. You’ll ensure every engineer on your teams understands how their workimpacts those analysts’ ability to defend their organizations. Customer empathyisn’t a slide in a deck—it’s a daily practice.Technical partnership. You’ll be a trusted partner in shaping Exabeam’stechnical direction. Your engineering directors bring deep technical rigor toarchitectural decisions. Your role is to ensure the organizationalconditions—team health, clarity of priorities, engineering culture, operationaldiscipline—that allow strong technical decisions to be made well and executedwith excellence.Who You AreWe evaluate candidates on four dimensions—drive, attitude, character, andtechnical aptitude—and we weight them deliberately. Technical depth matters, butwe’ve learned that the qualities which make engineering organizationsextraordinary can’t be taught. The right person for this role wins on who theyare before what they know.DriveYou lead with relentless curiosity and urgency. You’re energized by hardproblems and won’t rest until you’ve found the right answer, not just agood-enough one. You set ambitious goals for yourself and your teams, and youpursue them with the sustained intensity that building a market-leading platformdemands. You have a builder’s mentality—you derive energy from creating thingsthat matter, from teams to processes to products. When obstacles appear, youfind a way through. When the path is unclear, you move forward anyway—and bringpeople with you.AttitudeYou bring an entrepreneurial spirit and the grit to match. You approachcompeting priorities and distributed team dynamics with optimism andadaptability. You don’t wait for permission to improve things, and you don’taccept “that’s how we’ve always done it” as an answer. You see engineeringculture as a competitive advantage and invest in it deliberately—throughexperimentation, creative problem-solving, knowledge sharing, and celebration ofgreat work. You elevate the energy of every room you’re in and every team youlead. People feel more capable, more confident, and more connected after workingwith you.CharacterThis is the dimension we care about most deeply. Your character is your callingcard. You lead with integrity, earn trust through transparency, and holdyourself to the same standards you set for your teams.You build psychologically safe environments where engineers bring their fullselves to work, take smart risks, speak up when something feels wrong, and learnfrom failure without fear. You are fiercely protective of team health—youaddress toxic dynamics early, you advocate for your people in rooms they’re notin, and you create the conditions where collaboration happens naturally ratherthan by mandate.You understand that trust is the highest-leverage investment a leader can make.You extend it generously and earn it consistently. You communicate honestlyabout trade-offs, timelines, and technical debt, even when the truth isuncomfortable. You share context openly because you believe informed teams makebetter decisions than directed ones.When a team member is struggling, they feel your genuine investment in theirgrowth. When an engineering manager needs coaching, you give them the honestfeedback that helps them get better—not the comfortable feedback that keeps thepeace.You’ve built teams that people talk about years later. Not because of what theyshipped—although they shipped great things—but because of what it felt like tobe part of something meaningful.Technical AptitudeYou have enough technical depth to earn credibility with senior engineers andmake sound judgment calls about engineering direction. You don’t need to be thedeepest technical person in the room—your engineering directors own thatlane—but you understand the landscape well enough to ask the right questions,recognize strong technical thinking, and ensure engineering decisions align withproduct and business outcomes. You bring intellectual curiosity about AI/ML,behavioral analytics, and the evolving cybersecurity threat landscape, and youcreate space for your teams to explore and innovate in these areas.What You BringRequired Experience * 10+ years of progressive leadership in SaaS or platform organizations, including 5+ years leading teams of 50+ people across multiple product areas. Your background may span engineering, product operations, technical program management, or general management—what matters is that you’ve led the delivery of complex SaaS products at scale. * Demonstrated culture builder —You’ve built team cultures that attract and retain top talent through a genuine sense of purpose, psychological safety, and shared ownership of outcomes. You can point to specific teams you’ve built, practices you’ve introduced, or cultural transformations you’ve led that materially improved retention, engagement, and output. References will confirm it. * Trust-based leadership track record — you’ve led through influence and earned authority, not positional power. You’ve navigated organizational change, competing priorities, or rapid growth by building coalitions, maintaining transparency, and keeping teams aligned through uncertainty. * Global distributed team leadership — you’ve successfully led organizations spanning multiple geographies and time zones. You understand the intentional communication, organizational design, and cultural investment required to make distributed teams exceptional rather than merely functional. * Strong customer experience and success orientation — you’ve worked in organizations where the teams building the product are deeply connected to customer outcomes. You’ve built processes that translate customer pain into priorities and partnered closely with Customer Success, Support, and Product teams. * SaaS platform fluency — you understand the operational realities of running a cloud-native SaaS platform: release cadences, reliability expectations, observability, incident management, and the relationship between platform health and customer trust. You’ve led teams through the pressures of maintaining uptime while shipping innovation. * Observability and operational excellence mindset — whether from building observability practices or embedding SRE-driven culture, you understand that production insight is the foundation of product quality and customer trust. Valued Experience * Background in cybersecurity, SIEM, UEBA, or security operations—understanding the adversarial nature of the space and the operational pressures facing customer SOC teams * Experience leading within complex systems environments—large-scale data platforms, event-driven architectures, or high-throughput distributed systems where operational complexity is a defining challenge * Background in observability, APM, monitoring, or IT operations management platforms—the technical and operational parallels to SIEM are strong * Experience with AI/ML-driven product organizations, particularly those applying behavioral analytics, anomaly detection, or agentic AI to customer workflows * Familiarity with cloud-native infrastructure at scale (GCP, AWS, or Azure), particularly as it relates to cost optimization, reliability, and data processing * Experience in PE-backed or high-growth environments where operational efficiency, measurable outcomes, and disciplined execution are expected * Track record navigating organizational transformation—scaling teams, integrating acquisitions, or evolving operating models while maintaining team health and delivery velocity Why This Role, Why NowExabeam sits at a rare inflection point. The SIEM market is undergoing its mostsignificant transformation in a decade—driven by AI, cloud migration, and theconvergence of security operations platforms. As the largest independentpure-play SIEM provider, Exabeam has the AI heritage, product breadth, andmarket position to lead this transformation. * AI is in the DNA, not bolted on. Exabeam was the first to apply machine learning to SIEM. Today, our Nova agentic AI platform and Agent Behavior Analytics are defining the next generation of threat detection. * The leadership partnership is real. This is a technically engaged leadership team where your ownership of people, culture, and organizational health complements deep technical direction from the engineering directors. You won’t be asked to be everything—you’ll be asked to be the best at what matters most. * The products you build protect people. Every feature your teams ship helps customer SOC analysts detect and respond to threats faster. The stakes are tangible, the impact is measurable, and the mission is clear. * The scale is right for real leadership. With ~50 engineers, you’ll have enough organizational complexity to be strategic and enough proximity to your people to know them, develop them, and build something meaningful together. How We WorkThis is a remote-first position based in the United States. You would travelquarterly for leadership offsites, team gatherings, and customerengagement—typically 4–6 trips per year. We believe the best leaders can befound anywhere, and we invest in intentional in-person time to build the trustand relationships that make distributed teams thriveThe annual starting salary for this position is between $257,000- $290,000annually depending on experience and other qualifications of the successfulcandidate.Bring your Whole Self to Work!Diversity, equity, and inclusion are at the core of who we are. At Exabeam, weknow that diverse perspectives spark innovation, improve creativity, andposition our team for success. Creating a culture where all are welcomed,valued, and empowered to achieve their full potential is important to who we aretoday and in the future. We hire the best of the best and do not discriminatebased on race, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, identity, or otherpersonal factorsExabeam is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equalopportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, gender, genderidentity, genetic information, parental or pregnancy status, national origin,sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, or Veteranstatus.Show more Show less

job_description: About the projects:We are building LLM evaluation and training datasets to train LLM to work onrealistic software engineering problems. This role involves hands-on software engineering work, includingdevelopment environment automation, issue triaging, and evaluating test coverageand qualityWhy Join Us?Turing is one of the world’s fastest-growing AI companies accelerating theadvancement and deployment of powerful AI systems. You’ll be at the forefront ofevaluating how LLMs interact with real code, influencing the future ofAI-assisted software development. This is a unique opportunity to blendpractical software engineering with AI research.What does day-to-day look like: * Analyze and triage GitHub issues across trending open-source libraries. * Set up and configure code repositories, including Dockerization and environment setup. * Evaluating unit test coverage and quality. * Modify and run codebases locally to assess LLM performance in bug-fixing scenarios. * Collaborate with researchers to design and identify repositories and issues that are challenging for LLMs. * Opportunities to lead a team of junior engineers to collaborate on projects.Required Skills: * Minimum 3+ years of overall experience * Strong experience with at least one of the following languages: Rust * Proficiency with Git, Docker, and basic software pipeline setup. * Ability to understand and navigate complex codebases. * Comfortable running, modifying, and testing real-world projects locally. * Experience contributing to or evaluating open-source projects is a plus.Nice to Have: * Previous participation in LLM research or evaluation projects. * Experience building or testing developer tools or automation agents. * Perks of Freelancing With Turing: * Work in a fully remote environment. * Opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI projects with leading LLM companies.Offer Details: * Commitments Required: At least 4 hours per day and minimum 20 hours per week with overlap of 4 hours with PST.
